title: Boat Rental in Accra: Prices, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Accra offers a unique way to explore Ghana's coastline, with leisurely trips from Labadi Beach to the Tema fishing harbour. Prices for a half-day excursion on a small motorboat start around 250 EUR, while full-day charters on a Maiora 26m can exceed 2,500 EUR. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators across the region.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Accra?

Renting a boat in Accra typically costs between 150 and 3,000 EUR per day, depending on vessel size and crew. For example, a shared tour on a speedboat may cost around 75 EUR per person, while a private crewed yacht like an Azimut 50 can be 2,000 EUR. Always compare verified listings, such as the Maiora 26m listing on BoatHire24, to find transparent pricing. You can boat rental Accra for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Accra

Most boat trips depart from Labadi Beach, Kokrobite Beach, or the Tema Fishing Harbour. Sakumono Lagoon offers calmer waters for beginners, while Ada Foah, about 55 nautical miles east, is popular for multi-day excursions. The Port of Tema is the main commercial harbour, and Bojo Beach provides a more secluded launch point. Each location has varying facilities, so confirm mooring beforehand.

Which boat to rent in Accra

Choose from a range of vessels to suit your group and style. The following list highlights popular options with indicative prices for half-day hire.

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for a quick coastal spin, up to 8 people, from 250 EUR.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), stable floor for families, up to 12 guests, from 800 EUR.
  3. Cabin cruiser (e.g. Fairline 46), comfortable for day trips with overnight option, up to 8 guests, from 1,200 EUR.
  4. Luxury yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m), fully crewed with fine dining, up to 12 guests, from 2,500 EUR.
  5. Sailboat (e.g. Sunseeker 32), for sailing enthusiasts, up to 6 guests, from 400 EUR.

For an in-depth look at one of these options, review the Maiora 26m listing on BoatHire24, which details its amenities and crew. One option worth a look is the Maiora 26m listing.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Accra?

No, you do not need a licence to rent a boat in Accra if you hire a skippered vessel. Ghana requires a local licence for self-skippered boats, but most rental companies provide a captain. The operator confirms exact requirements during booking. For bareboat charters, you must hold a recognised international certificate and, depending on the boat, may need a local permit.

Aim to depart before 10am to catch the calm morning conditions; the afternoon Harmattan breeze can create choppy seas from December to February.

When is the best season for boat rental in Accra?

The best season for boating in Accra is from November to April, during the dry Harmattan period. The sea is calmest between December and February, with steady south-westerly winds. From June to October, heavy rains and stronger currents can disrupt trips. Always check the local forecast and book flexible cancellations.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150, 3,000 EUR per day
  • Peak months: November to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4, 5 hours) typical, full-day also available
  • Max guests: up to 12 on large yachts
  • Currency: EUR (most operators accept it)

Questions about boat rental in Accra

Can I rent a boat for a sunset cruise in Accra?

Yes, many operators offer sunset cruises departing from Labadi Beach, usually lasting three hours and costing around 200 EUR for a group of six.

Are there any all-inclusive charter options?

Several luxury yachts, such as the Maiora 26m, include food, drinks and fuel in the daily rate, but always confirm the inclusions with the host.

What should I bring on a boat trip from Accra?

Pack sunscreen, a hat, swimwear and a windbreaker, as the Harmattan breeze can be cool even on sunny days.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Accra alone?

It is generally safe, but always choose a verified operator through a trusted platform and share your itinerary with someone ashore.

Before you finalise your trip, compare options on the boat rental Accra page to secure the best vessel for your needs. By 2026, the platform expects to feature over 50 boats in the area, making it easy to plan a memorable day on the water.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Accra and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
